skill.name: pathway-introduction-expert
skill.description: Generates comprehensive academic introductions for biological pathways, including signaling processes, markers, and inhibitors. Use when the user asks to introduce a pathway, molecule, or gene.
skill.summary: Generates structured academic introductions for biological pathways with markers, agonists, and inhibitor details for research writing.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 33 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The 12 HIGH-severity 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings reference JSON evaluation notes, an argparse description string, and markdown text with no actual cryptographic code. The 19 MEDIUM-severity 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code blocks showing benign documentation examples (python scripts/validate_skill.py --help). The 2 LOW network findings refer to a GitHub source attribution link. The validate_skill.py script is a trivial argparse helper with no dangerous functionality. No prompt injection or malicious intent detected. Residual risk factors are external_commands (documentation of benign bash commands) and network (GitHub URL), both contextually appropriate for this academic writing skill.

content.user_title: Generate Academic Introductions for Biological Pathways
content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours crafting thorough pathway introductions for papers, grants, and theses. This skill generates structured, comprehensive introductions for any biological pathway, including signaling processes, markers, agonists, and inhibitors, following a consistent academic format.
actual_capabilities: Generates detailed introductions for any biological signaling pathway including protein interactions and signaling processes
Lists key markers and genes with their detection forms such as Western blot, IHC, and ELISA
Identifies known agonists and inhibitors for specific signaling pathways
Provides related pathway suggestions when given a molecule or gene as input
Produces output in Chinese language following a strict structured format
Follows a consistent section order for reproducible academic writing
limitations: Cannot verify accuracy of pathway information - always cross-reference with primary literature
Does not include references or citations in generated output
Limited to the knowledge cutoff of the underlying AI model for pathway data
Output is primarily formatted for Chinese-language academic writing contexts

use_cases.title: Graduate Student Literature Review
use_cases.description: A graduate student studying cell biology needs a comprehensive introduction to the MAPK signaling pathway for their thesis literature review chapter, including markers, agonists, and detection methods.

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Background Section
use_cases.description: A postdoctoral researcher writing a grant proposal on Wnt signaling needs a structured pathway overview including markers, agonists, and inhibitors to include in the background section.

use_cases.title: Clinical Research Molecular Background
use_cases.description: A clinical researcher investigating a gene's role in cancer needs related pathways and marker information to build the molecular background section of a manuscript.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Pathway Introduction
prompt_templates.prompt: Introduce the [pathway name] signaling pathway. Include its general function, key markers, known agonists, and inhibitors.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick overview request for a known signaling pathway

prompt_templates.title: Molecule-to-Pathway Exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: I am studying the gene [gene name]. List the top 5 related pathways, then provide a detailed introduction to the most relevant one including markers and detection methods.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting from a gene or molecule to discover related pathways

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Analysis with Detection Methods
prompt_templates.prompt: Provide a detailed introduction to the [pathway name]. For each marker gene, include its function and detection form such as Western blot for phosphorylation or IHC for expression. Also list all known agonists and inhibitors.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed research support with specific detection method requirements

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Pathway Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the [pathway A] and [pathway B] signaling pathways. For each, provide a general introduction covering protein interactions, key markers with detection forms, and known agonists and inhibitors. Highlight their crosstalk points.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced comparative analysis for grant or manuscript preparation

output_examples.output:
I. Pathway Introduction: The MAPK (Mitogen-Activated Protein Kinase) pathway is a highly conserved signaling cascade that transmits extracellular signals to the nucleus, regulating cell proliferation, differentiation, and apoptosis. The core pathway involves three sequentially activated kinases: MAPKKK, MAPKK, and MAPK. Key subfamilies include ERK1/2, JNK, and p38 MAPK.

II. Markers and Key Genes: 1. ERK1/2; Key effector of growth factor signaling; Detection: Phosphorylation-specific Western blot. 2. JNK; Stress-responsive kinase; Detection: Phospho-JNK antibody in IHC. 3. p38; Inflammatory response mediator; Detection: Phospho-p38 ELISA.

III. Agonists and Inhibitors: 1. Agonists: EGF, NGF, FGF, PMA. 2. Inhibitors: U0126 (MEK inhibitor), PD98059 (MEK inhibitor), SB203580 (p38 inhibitor).

output_examples.input: What pathways are related to the TP53 gene?
output_examples.output: TP53 is a critical tumor suppressor gene. Top 5 related pathways: 1. p53 Signaling Pathway (most relevant), 2. DNA Damage Response Pathway, 3. Apoptosis Signaling Pathway, 4. Cell Cycle Regulation Pathway, 5. PI3K/AKT/mTOR Pathway. Selected: p53 Signaling Pathway. I. Introduction: The p53 signaling pathway is a central tumor suppressor mechanism responding to cellular stresses including DNA damage and hypoxia. II. Markers: MDM2 (Western blot), CDKN1A/p21 (IHC), BAX (Western blot). III. Agonists and Inhibitors: Nutlin-3 (MDM2 inhibitor), PFT-alpha (p53 inhibitor).

output_examples.input: Describe the markers and inhibitors of the PI3K/AKT pathway
output_examples.output:
I. Pathway Introduction: The PI3K/AKT signaling pathway regulates cell survival, growth, and metabolism. Activation begins with receptor tyrosine kinase stimulation, leading to PI3K phosphorylation of PIP2 to PIP3, which recruits AKT for activation by PDK1 and mTORC2.

II. Markers: 1. PI3K p110-alpha; Detection: IHC for expression. 2. AKT; Detection: Phospho-AKT Ser473 Western blot. 3. PTEN; Detection: IHC for PTEN loss. 4. mTOR; Detection: Phospho-mTOR Ser2448 IHC.

III. Agonists and Inhibitors: 1. Agonists: Insulin, IGF-1, EGF. 2. Inhibitors: Wortmannin (PI3K), LY294002 (PI3K), MK-2206 (AKT), Rapamycin (mTOR).

best_practices: Verify all pathway information against recent primary literature before using in publications or grant applications.
Specify the biological context such as species, tissue type, or disease state for more accurate pathway details.
Use the molecule-to-pathway workflow when starting from a single gene or protein to discover related signaling networks.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated output as clinically actionable without verification by a domain expert.
Do not request introductions for hypothetical or non-existent pathways as the skill may produce fabricated information.
Do not use this skill as a replacement for curated pathway databases such as KEGG or Reactome for data mining.

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for any biological pathway?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as the pathway is within the AI model's training data. Common pathways like MAPK, PI3K/AKT, Wnt, and NF-kB are well supported.

faq.question: Does the skill include references or citations?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates introductions based on the AI model's knowledge. Add your own citations from primary literature.

faq.question: Why is the output in Chinese?
faq.answer: The skill template is designed for Chinese-language academic writing. Modify the output format instructions in the skill definition to use other languages.

faq.question: Can this skill help with clinical diagnosis?
faq.answer: No. This skill is for academic and educational purposes only. Do not use it for clinical decision-making.

faq.question: How detailed are the marker detection methods?
faq.answer: The skill lists common detection forms such as Western blot, IHC, ELISA, and qPCR for each marker. For experimental planning, consult published protocols.
